entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: An important legume food crop grown primarily by smallholder farmers in semi-arid tropical regions.

entities.name: ILLUMINA NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING
entities.description: A sequencing platform used to generate 237.2 Gb of sequence for the pigeonpea genome.

entities.name: SANGER-BASED BAC END SEQUENCES
entities.description: Bacterial artificial chromosome end sequences used in conjunction with other data to assemble the pigeonpea genome.

entities.name: GENETIC MAP
entities.description: A map used to assist in the assembly of the pigeonpea genome scaffolds.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA GENOME
entities.description: The genetic material of pigeonpea, assembled into scaffolds representing 72.7% (605.78 Mb) of the total 833.07 Mb.

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: Predicted 48,680 genetic units within the pigeonpea genome.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE-RELATED GENES
entities.description: Specific gene families that played a role in the domestication of pigeonpea and the evolution of its ancestors.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: An agronomically important trait related to the ability to survive and produce under water-limited conditions.

entities.name: DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of adapting wild pigeonpea ancestors for agricultural use.

entities.name: EVOLUTION
entities.description: The biological development of pigeonpea and its ancestors over time.

entities.name: SEGMENTAL DUPLICATION
entities.description: Events where specific segments of the genome are duplicated.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE DUPLICATION
entities.description: Large-scale duplication events of the entire genome, observed in soybean but not recently in pigeonpea.

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: A legume crop used as a comparison for genome-wide duplication events.

entities.name: AGRONOMICALLY IMPORTANT TRAITS
entities.description: Genetic characteristics of pigeonpea that are valuable for agricultural improvement.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food in developing countries.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Geographic areas where pigeonpea is primarily grown.

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions where improved pigeonpea varieties can enhance food security.
